emergency travel support hours when book.title: Why am I receiving a new hotel confirmation I did not request?
emergency travel support hours when book.text:
Why am I receiving a new hotel confirmation I did not request?
Why am I receiving a new hotel confirmation I did not request? Amazon Travel & Events (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/) uses automated auditing technology that monitors your hotel reservations after booking to find lower rates. When a better rate becomes available, the system automatically rebooks your hotel to capture savings. What stays the same Your hotel property Your check-in and check-out dates Your room type and preferences All other reservation details What changes Your confirmation number (you receive a new one) Your rate (it is lower) Why this happens Hotel rates fluctuate due to demand, cancellations, and promotional pricing. The automated system monitors for price drops and secures savings automatically. What you need to do No action is needed from you. Rebooking happens automatically. Some exemptions may apply based on specific booking circumstances. Always use your new confirmation number when checking in or contacting the hotel.

emergency travel support hours when book.title: Do I need my manager's approval to book travel?
emergency travel support hours when book.text:
Do I need my manager's approval to book travel?
Do I need my manager's approval to book travel? No. Manager approval is not required for most business travel bookings through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) . Refer to sections 3.1 Travel Approval (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#c4c58a98-350a-90bb-e842-09df66482170) and 3.2 Travel Planning (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#cec58a98-3503-4372-17e2-ee56a6f0d879) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. Automatic notifications Your manager receives your Itinerary automatically when you complete a booking. This notification is for awareness purposes only and does not require action.

emergency travel support hours when book.title: How Do I Cancel or Modify a Hotel Booking? (Regional)
emergency travel support hours when book.text:
How Do I Cancel or Modify a Hotel Booking? (Regional)
How do I cancel or modify a hotel booking? (Regional) Use the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to modify or cancel your hotel booking. Most hotel changes can be handled directly through self-service. Refer to section 3.10 Itinerary Changes and Cancellations (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. Requirements before modifying Confirm your reservation meets all of the following requirements: You have not checked into the hotel. Your request falls within the cancellation policy guidelines. The hotel has not been paid for in advance. If your reservation does not meet these requirements, contact Travel Counselor (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance. How to modify your hotel booking There are two types of hotel changes: Modify the dates of your current hotel reservation. Book an entirely new hotel (requires cancellation of the original reservation). In both cases, book the new hotel or new dates first, then cancel the old reservation. This ensures you do not lose the current reservation if the preferred hotel is sold out.

emergency travel support hours when book.title: How Do I Cancel or Modify a Hotel Booking? (Global Access)
emergency travel support hours when book.text:
How Do I Cancel or Modify a Hotel Booking? (Global Access)
How do I cancel or modify a hotel booking? (Global Access) Use the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to modify or cancel your hotel booking. Most hotel changes can be handled directly through self-service. Refer to section 3.10 Itinerary Changes and Cancellations (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. Requirements before modifying Confirm your reservation meets all of the following requirements: You have not checked into the hotel. Your request falls within the cancellation policy guidelines. The hotel has not been paid for in advance. If your reservation does not meet these requirements, contact Travel Counselor (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance. How to modify your hotel booking Log in to the Self-Service Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) . Navigate to Trips > Upcoming and select your trip. Find your hotel reservation and select Modify (or Cancel). Make your changes: Update check-in and check-out dates by editing the date fields and selecting Check Availability, or Select a different room type or rate. Review the changes and any price differences on the Checkout page. Select Book Hotel to confirm your modifications. Watch this quick video: Modify Your Flight & Hotel Booking (https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1594156) .

emergency travel support hours when book.title: Can I earn personal miles or loyalty points when traveling for business?
emergency travel support hours when book.text:
Can I earn personal miles or loyalty points when traveling for business?
Can I earn personal miles or loyalty points when traveling for business? Yes. You can earn personal loyalty miles and points when traveling for business through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) . This applies to airlines, hotels, and rental cars. Refer to section 3.12 Loyalty Programs (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-12) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. Eligibility restrictions Employees based in Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Israel are not eligible to participate in supplier point programs due to payroll tax compliance requirements. How to earn miles Locate more information on the Amazon Travel & Events Preferred Travel Providers and Promotions Wiki (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/BusinessTravelPromotions/) . Sign up for loyalty programs. Add your loyalty program numbers to your Travel Profile. Miles and rewards are automatically credited to your personal account after each trip. Important requirements Ensure your loyalty numbers are accurate in your Travel Profile to avoid missing credits. Your Travel Profile name and your loyalty program name must match exactly. Contact the travel provider directly if miles are not credited after your trip.

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: How Do I Set Up My Travel Profile? (India)
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
How Do I Set Up My Travel Profile? (India)
How do I set up my Travel Profile? You must create a Travel Profile in the Self-Service Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) before booking business travel. Your Travel Profile securely stores your personal information, passport details, payment method, and travel preferences. Refer to section 3.4 Traveler Profiles (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#52c58a98-3509-0d43-4f83-20fcd24c2fec) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. What your Travel Profile stores Personal information: mobile phone number and date of birth (varies by country) Passport details (for international travel) Corporate or personal credit card (required to secure hotel reservations) Travel preferences and loyalty program numbers (optional) Important: Some information will be pre-populated for you—verify that your name exactly matches your government-issued ID. If your name exceeds 58 characters (the 58-character limit includes: salutation, space/dot , first name, middle name, and last name), you will need to book travel arrangements with a Travel Counselor. You will not be able to make travel arrangements online. Quick start guides Travel Profile Quick Start Guide for Travelers (India) (https://amazon.sharepoint.com/sites/AmazonTravelEventsPublicResources/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public%2FAmazon%5FTripsource%5FIndia%5FProfile%5FQSG%2D2025%2DFINAL%20%281%29%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public&p=true&ga=1) Travel Profile Quick Start Guide for Travel Arrangers (India) (https://amazon.sharepoint.com/sites/AmazonTravelEventsPublicResources/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public%2FAmazon%5FTripsource%5FIndia%5FTravel%20Arranger%5FQSG%2D2025%2DFINAL%20%281%29%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public&p=true&ga=1) How to book after profile setup Standard bookings: Log in to the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and search for flights, hotels, car rentals, or rail options. Last-minute bookings (within 6 hours of departure): Contact a Travel Counselor (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) during business hours. For same-day travel outside the 6-hour window, book online through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) .

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: Why am I receiving a new hotel confirmation I did not request?
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
Why am I receiving a new hotel confirmation I did not request?
Why am I receiving a new hotel confirmation I did not request? Amazon Travel & Events (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/) uses automated auditing technology that monitors your hotel reservations after booking to find lower rates. When a better rate becomes available, the system automatically rebooks your hotel to capture savings. What stays the same Your hotel property Your check-in and check-out dates Your room type and preferences All other reservation details What changes Your confirmation number (you receive a new one) Your rate (it is lower) Why this happens Hotel rates fluctuate due to demand, cancellations, and promotional pricing. The automated system monitors for price drops and secures savings automatically. What you need to do No action is needed from you. Rebooking happens automatically. Some exemptions may apply based on specific booking circumstances. Always use your new confirmation number when checking in or contacting the hotel.

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: Where do I locate Travel Counselor contact information?
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
Where do I locate Travel Counselor contact information?
Where do I locate Travel Counselor contact information? Find Travel Counselor (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) contact information — including phone, email, chat, and hours by country — on the Amazon Travel & Events Wiki (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) . Travel Counselors are available 24/7, 365 days per year in most countries. Contact a Travel Counselor (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) from the phone number listed in your Travel Profile. This ensures proper call routing and identification. Chat is available within the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) in most countries. Chat does not support new reservations. Before you call: Tips for faster service Have your Trip ID ready — this helps Travel Counselors assist you more quickly Update your contact information — ensure your Travel Profile includes your current phone number for proper call routing Departing within 72 hours? — you are automatically prioritized and moved to the top of the call queue Do not want to wait on hold? — use the Callback Feature International calling Travelers outside their home country must have an international calling plan to reach the designated country contact number. If you do not have an international calling plan, use chat or email for assistance.

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: I need to change my travel plans (Regional)
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
I need to change my travel plans (Regional)
I need to change my travel plans Use the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to cancel or rebook your trip. Self-service supports cancellations and modifications for most bookings. Refer to section 3.10 Itinerary Changes and Cancellations (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. How to cancel or modify your flight Navigate to your Upcoming Trips in the Travel section of your home page. If online self-service changes are not available for your booking, contact a Travel Counselor (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance. If further support is needed, visit the Amazon Travel & Events Wiki (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for more information.

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: I Accidentally Used My Corporate Card for a Personal Expense. What Should I Do? - Global
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
I Accidentally Used My Corporate Card for a Personal Expense. What Should I Do? - Global
Repaying Personal Expenses (Emburse) If you accidentally used your corporate card for a personal expense, need to split an expense as partially personal, or otherwise need to repay Amazon, you will need to take the following steps to submit an expense report to reconcile the expense. See the How to Submit Expenses user guide (https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses) for how to navigate the Expense Tool (https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) . If you used your corporate card for personal use. Common Scenario: Hey, it happens, you accidentally swiped your corporate card instead of your personal card. Find the corporate card charge in the Expense Tool (https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet If the entire amount is personal and non-reimbursable, select the Expense Type “Personal Expense”. Fill out the required fields. Continue building your Expense Report and submit. If you need to split an expense as partially personal. Common Scenario: You purchased multiple items in the same transaction but some of the expenses are ineligible for reimbursement per policy. Find the corporate card charge in the Expense Tool (https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet or create a new expense by uploading the receipt. Apply the appropriate expense type as per the nature of the expense and fill out the required fields. Under Allocation, click Add Allocation and select “PERSONAL Non-Reimbursable Personal Expense” Adjust the amounts to reflect what amount is personal. Continue building your Expense Report and submit. If you were reimbursed for an expense in error and now need to pay back Amazon. Common Scenario: You created a corporate card expense from scratch instead of waiting for it to appear in Concur. The system treats it as an out-of-pocket reimbursement and sends money to your personal account (as listed in Amazon Touchstone). Meanwhile, the actual corporate card charge remains "unsubmitted", which can lead to reminder emails - and even card suspension - if not corrected. Find the corporate card charge in the Expense Tool (https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet. If the entire amount is personal and non-reimbursable, select the Expense Type “Personal Expense” Fill out the required fields and add a comment explaining your previous incorrect submission, including the original expense report number. Continue building your Expense Report and submit. What Happens Next When you submit expenses as personal, you authorize Amazon to deduct the amount from future reimbursements or the account you have set up in Amazon Touchstone (https://touchstone.amazon.com/) . Repeated personal use on a corporate card may result in card suspension, so use your corporate card carefully. For additional guidance visit the Employee Expense & Corporate Card RIVER (https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER) .

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: Are Mobile Phone Expenses Reimbursable? - Global
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
Are Mobile Phone Expenses Reimbursable? - Global
Are mobile phone expenses reimbursable? For employees L4-L8, Amazon reimburses up to $50 USD per month of your personal mobile phone base plan. Exceptions apply for L1-3 and L10+. How to get reimbursed for your mobile phone bill: When submitting in the Expense Tool (https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) , select "Mobile Phone" as the Expense Type, enter your total base plan cost, and indicate your business use percentage (25% for infrequent use, 50% for mixed use, 75% for significant use, or 100% for primarily work use). The system calculates your reimbursement automatically, capped at $50. You'll need to attach your full itemized bill showing your name and/or phone number. If you use your phone for 100% business purposes, Amazon recommends getting an Amazon-provided mobile phone (https://my.it.a2z.com/articles/hardware/company-mobile-device/) if it is available in your country. Need to expense extra charges beyond your base plan? You can get reimbursed up to $150/month for incremental work-related costs like international roaming, travel daily fees, or mobile hotspots when corporate Wi-Fi isn't available. Submit these using the "Travel Related – Incremental Mobile Fees" Expense Type with detailed line-item documentation. Consult Section 12.6 of the Amazon Global Travel and Expense Policy (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) before submitting your mobile phone expenses for reimbursement to ensure compliance and eligibility.

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: What Hotel Accommodations am I Allowed to Book?
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
What Hotel Accommodations am I Allowed to Book?
What hotel accommodations am I allowed to book? Book the lowest refundable standard hotel room rate on a single-occupancy basis near your work destination through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) . All business travel must be booked within Amazon Travel & Events. Refer to section 5.0 Accomodation (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dec58a98-346b-920c-1662-8dbc2b8e05fb) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. Hotel location Book accommodations close to your work destination through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) or a Travel Counselor (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) . Hotel room type Amazon reimburses the lowest refundable standard hotel room rate on a single-occupancy basis. Why book Amazon preferred hotels Amazon negotiates with leading hotels conveniently located near Amazon offices. Preferred hotels offer: Competitive rates Flexible cancellation policies Loyalty program eligibility (not available in all countries or with all providers) Safety and security vetting While you may occasionally find a less expensive non-negotiated option, these rates may come with restrictions. Booking preferred rates contributes to overall cost savings across all travelers and provides greater flexibility if your plans change. Found a cheaper rate outside the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) ? Contact a Travel Counselor (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) to check if the same room/rate offer can be booked through Amazon Travel & Events. The offer must match the original room type and rate exactly. Long-term stay accommodation (7+ nights) When staying in the same location for 7 or more nights, Corporate Serviced Apartments can offer additional space, comfort, amenities, and savings. Unit types Select a studio apartment/flat or one-bedroom apartment/flat, whichever is less expensive. Approval requirements Long-term stay accommodation (Corporate Serviced Apartments only) requires L8+ approval. Shared economy properties The use of shared apartments or rental homes (including Airbnb) is strongly discouraged due to safety and security concerns. Use shared economy properties only when no suitable hotel or Corporate Serviced Apartment is available.

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: Why is my booking out of policy?
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
Why is my booking out of policy?
Why is my booking out of policy? The "Out of Policy" flag in the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) helps you identify options that comply with the Global Travel and Expense Policy (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#1) . It does not prevent you from completing your booking. What the Global Travel and Expense Policy considers Lowest Logical Fare in economy with a seat assignment Lowest hotel rates Preferred car rates for the approved car type Out of Policy vs. Policy Violation Out of Policy: Your selection exceeds policy guidelines but may be justified for business reasons Yes — provide a reason for your selection. Policy Violation: Your selection violates a hard rule (for example, booking first class). No — the booking cannot be completed. When an out-of-policy booking is justified You may proceed with an out-of-policy booking when there is a valid business reason. Examples include: A hotel that is out of policy but closer to your business meetings An intermediate SUV during inclement weather for safety reasons Provide a brief reason when prompted. This serves as an audit tool to ensure flexibility while maintaining safety and frugality.

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: Am I allowed to upgrade my fare class? (Regional)
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
Am I allowed to upgrade my fare class? (Regional)
Am I allowed to upgrade my fare class? (Regional) Yes. Amazon reimburses only the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class. You must pay the difference between the policy-approved fare and the upgraded class out of pocket. Refer to section 4.1 Airline Cabin Class (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#4-1) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. How to upgrade Contact a Travel Counselor and request a personal upgrade for your trip. Pay for the entire ticket cost upfront using your own payment method. Obtain documentation showing the price difference between the Lowest Logical Fare and the upgraded class. When you expense your trip, claim reimbursement only for the documented Lowest Logical Fare noted by the travel services provider. Upgrade fees and premium seating fees are not reimbursable. Manager notification Your manager receives an email notification indicating you have added a personal upgrade. No manager action is required.

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: Do I need my manager's approval to book travel?
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
Do I need my manager's approval to book travel?
Do I need my manager's approval to book travel? No. Manager approval is not required for most business travel bookings through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) . Refer to sections 3.1 Travel Approval (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#c4c58a98-350a-90bb-e842-09df66482170) and 3.2 Travel Planning (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#cec58a98-3503-4372-17e2-ee56a6f0d879) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. Automatic notifications Your manager receives your Itinerary automatically when you complete a booking. This notification is for awareness purposes only and does not require action.

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: Do I Need Rental Car Insurance for Work Travel?
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
Do I Need Rental Car Insurance for Work Travel?
Do I need rental car insurance for work travel? Amazon provides Collision Damage Waiver (CDW) and Liability Damage Waiver (LDW) insurance for business rentals when you book through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and use preferred suppliers. Refer to section 6.2 Car Rental Insurance (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#88c58a98-3515-6808-3fb9-b6456aec9e1b) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. What is covered Collision Damage Waiver (CDW) Liability Damage Waiver (LDW) Deductibles may apply based on rental location What to do at the rental counter Decline additional insurance at the rental counter unless legally required and not already included in your rate.

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: What Is the Deadline for Submitting Expenses? - Global
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
What Is the Deadline for Submitting Expenses? - Global
What is the deadline for submitting expenses? The Global Travel and Expense Policy (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) requires employees submit expense reports in the Expense Tool (https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) within 60 days of the transaction date. This applies to both out-of-pocket expenses (paid with cash or using your own credit card) and corporate card transactions (which will automatically populate in the Expense Tool (https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) within 5 business days). If you fail to submit corporate card transactions within 60 days, your corporate card may be suspended. Resubmitting if your expense report is rejected Most employee expense reports are processed and paid within 4 business days. If your expense report requires additional approvals or review, it could take longer than usual to be fully processed. Should you need to resubmit your expense report if it was initially rejected, the clock does not restart against the 60-day deadline. Need help? Review the How to Submit Expenses User Guide (https://w.amazon.com/bin/Concur_Global_Expense/User_Guide) before you submit an expense report to prevent errors that might lead to delays in reimbursement.

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: Do I need a visa for travel?
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
Do I need a visa for travel?
Do I need a visa for travel? You must complete a pre-travel immigration assessment through the International Business Travel Guided Experience before traveling internationally. This assessment determines your specific visa and immigration requirements based on your destination, citizenship, and trip details. Refer to section 3.9 Visas and Passports (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-9) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. What the assessment covers Visa requirements for your destination country Immigration documentation needed based on your citizenship Trip-specific requirements based on duration and purpose of travel

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: Are Meals Covered During Business Travel? - Global
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
Are Meals Covered During Business Travel? - Global
Are meals covered during business travel? Amazon has you covered for meals and snacks while you're traveling away from home for business. Per diem and regional guidance Visit the Travel Reimbursement Guide (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Concur_Global_Expense/Travel_Reimbursement/) to learn more about per diems and region-specific guidance for expense limits what is eligible for meal reimbursement when you travel. Tips for meal expense compliance Retain the itemized receipt that includes a breakout of the individual meal items to later submit with your expense report. For team meals when traveling , the most senior person should pick up the tab and list everyone who attended when submitting the expense. Only split the bill if each person plans to expense their own meal separately. Additional rules may apply if your meal includes a government official. Review the Gift Reporting Guidelines (https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/legal-bce/GiftReportingGuidelines) and Section 12.12 of Amazon's Global Travel and Expense Policy (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#12.1) and consult with your manager before you entertain a government official.

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: How Do I Cancel or Modify a Hotel Booking? (Global Access)
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
How Do I Cancel or Modify a Hotel Booking? (Global Access)
How do I cancel or modify a hotel booking? (Global Access) Use the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to modify or cancel your hotel booking. Most hotel changes can be handled directly through self-service. Refer to section 3.10 Itinerary Changes and Cancellations (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. Requirements before modifying Confirm your reservation meets all of the following requirements: You have not checked into the hotel. Your request falls within the cancellation policy guidelines. The hotel has not been paid for in advance. If your reservation does not meet these requirements, contact Travel Counselor (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance. How to modify your hotel booking Log in to the Self-Service Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) . Navigate to Trips > Upcoming and select your trip. Find your hotel reservation and select Modify (or Cancel). Make your changes: Update check-in and check-out dates by editing the date fields and selecting Check Availability, or Select a different room type or rate. Review the changes and any price differences on the Checkout page. Select Book Hotel to confirm your modifications. Watch this quick video: Modify Your Flight & Hotel Booking (https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1594156) .

When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.title: I need to change my travel plans (Global Access)
When I've got a same-day trip coming up,.text:
I need to change my travel plans (Global Access)
I need to change my travel plans Use the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to cancel or rebook your trip. Self-service supports cancellations and modifications for most bookings. Refer to section 3.10 Itinerary Changes and Cancellations (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. To cancel a flight Log in to the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) . Navigate to Trips > Upcoming and select your trip. Select Cancel next to the flight. Confirm the cancellation twice to complete. To modify a flight Select your segment and select Modify. Choose the flight to change. Update your details. Search for new flights and complete checkout. Watch this quick video: Modify Your Flight & Hotel (https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1594156) .

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: What Is the Process for Booking a Seat? (Global Access)
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
What Is the Process for Booking a Seat? (Global Access)
What is the process for booking a seat? Book the Lowest Logical Fare with a seat assignment and baggage included. The Self-Service Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) highlights in-policy fares for easy selection. Refer to section 4.7 Air Travel Reimbursable Incidentals (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#76c58a98-34c4-136c-3e99-4d007e22b32c) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. EMEA and APAC travelers: ancillary purchases Fares that include a standard seat or baggage may sometimes exceed the Lowest Logical Fare limits in EMEA and APAC regions. If this occurs: Purchase the seat or baggage separately as an ancillary item directly with the airline. Expense the additional cost separately. Save all receipts for any ancillary purchases. How to select a seat when included in your fare Seat maps are available during the shopping path only. Use them to compare seat availability across flights before making your selection. During booking A seat may be auto-assigned when you select a flight at checkout. You may be able to select a different seat — view the seat map at checkout and choose your preferred seat before completing your booking. After booking Change your seat once ticketed by: Navigating to Trips > My Trips in the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool Using the airline mobile app directly (varies by airline) Watch this quick video: How to Change Your Seat (https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1656533)

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: What Is The Process for Booking a Seat? (Regional)
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
What Is The Process for Booking a Seat? (Regional)
What is the process for booking a seat? Book the Lowest Logical Fare with a seat assignment and baggage included. The Self-Service Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) highlights in-policy fares for easy selection. Refer to section 4.7 Air Travel Reimbursable Incidentals (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#76c58a98-34c4-136c-3e99-4d007e22b32c) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. EMEA and APAC travelers: ancillary purchases Fares that include a standard seat or baggage may sometimes exceed the Lowest Logical Fare limits in EMEA and APAC regions. If this occurs: Purchase the seat or baggage separately as an ancillary item directly with the airline. Expense the additional cost separately. Save all receipts for any ancillary purchases.

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: What Flights Am I Allowed to Book?
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
What Flights Am I Allowed to Book?
What flights am I allowed to book? Book the Lowest Logical Fare in Coach/Economy Class that includes a seat assignment through the Self-Service Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) . Refer to section 4.1 Airline Cabin Class (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dec58a98-34c2-2f68-ea13-80ffad035827) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. Non-stop flight preference You may prioritize non-stop flights over connecting flights whenever possible and practical. Exception for non-stop flights If a non-stop flight is not the Lowest Logical Fare, consult with your manager to determine the most appropriate flight option, balancing frugality and well-being.

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: Am I allowed to upgrade my fare class? (Regional)
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
Am I allowed to upgrade my fare class? (Regional)
Am I allowed to upgrade my fare class? (Regional) Yes. Amazon reimburses only the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class. You must pay the difference between the policy-approved fare and the upgraded class out of pocket. Refer to section 4.1 Airline Cabin Class (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#4-1) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. How to upgrade Contact a Travel Counselor and request a personal upgrade for your trip. Pay for the entire ticket cost upfront using your own payment method. Obtain documentation showing the price difference between the Lowest Logical Fare and the upgraded class. When you expense your trip, claim reimbursement only for the documented Lowest Logical Fare noted by the travel services provider. Upgrade fees and premium seating fees are not reimbursable. Manager notification Your manager receives an email notification indicating you have added a personal upgrade. No manager action is required.

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: Why is my booking out of policy?
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
Why is my booking out of policy?
Why is my booking out of policy? The "Out of Policy" flag in the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) helps you identify options that comply with the Global Travel and Expense Policy (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#1) . It does not prevent you from completing your booking. What the Global Travel and Expense Policy considers Lowest Logical Fare in economy with a seat assignment Lowest hotel rates Preferred car rates for the approved car type Out of Policy vs. Policy Violation Out of Policy: Your selection exceeds policy guidelines but may be justified for business reasons Yes — provide a reason for your selection. Policy Violation: Your selection violates a hard rule (for example, booking first class). No — the booking cannot be completed. When an out-of-policy booking is justified You may proceed with an out-of-policy booking when there is a valid business reason. Examples include: A hotel that is out of policy but closer to your business meetings An intermediate SUV during inclement weather for safety reasons Provide a brief reason when prompted. This serves as an audit tool to ensure flexibility while maintaining safety and frugality.

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: Am I Allowed to Upgrade My Fare Class? (Global Access)
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
Am I Allowed to Upgrade My Fare Class? (Global Access)
Am I Allowed to Upgrade My Fare Class? (Global Access) Yes. Amazon reimburses only the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class. You must pay the difference between the policy-approved fare and the upgraded class out of pocket. Refer to section 4.1 Airline Cabin Class (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#4-1) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. How to upgrade Search and select your preferred upgraded cabin class in the Self-Service Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) (desktop only). The fare indicator displays the policy-approved amount and personal upgrade cost. On the Out of Policy pop-up, select Skip suggestions and proceed to the checkout page, then select Next. On the Checkout page, review the breakdown of the two amounts. Confirm your personal card is set as the form of payment. Acknowledge the booking contains a personal upgrade. Select Book flight to finalize your reservation. You pay the full amount upfront and submit the policy-approved portion for reimbursement. The invoice/Itinerary you receive includes a Personal Upgrade Summary for your Expense Report. Watch this quick video: How to Book a Personal Upgrade (https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1687601) . Manager notification Your manager receives an email notification indicating you have added a personal upgrade. No manager action is required.

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: Can I upgrade my fare using miles?
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
Can I upgrade my fare using miles?
Can I upgrade my fare using miles? Yes. After booking travel through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) , you may contact the airline directly to request an upgrade using your personal loyalty miles. Mileage upgrades cannot be processed through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) — you must request them directly with your airline. Refer to section 3.12 Loyalty Programs (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#c6c58a98-350b-aaa2-8c87-d9cb7f980da2) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. Important limitations Airline policies vary: Not all airlines allow upgrades with miles for tickets purchased through third-party booking platforms. Fare class restrictions: Basic economy and deeply discounted fares are typically ineligible for mileage upgrades. Verification required: Review your specific fare rules and restrictions before requesting an upgrade.

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: Where Can I Locate My Previously Booked Trips? (Regional)
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
Where Can I Locate My Previously Booked Trips? (Regional)
Where can I locate my previously booked trips? Select Trips Library from the main menu in the Self-Service Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to access current and past trips, including invoices. Archived or missing trips If a trip is no longer visible or has been archived, open a request through Travel RIVER (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to access your historical booking records. P rovider-managed bookings Bookings made directly through travel providers (including rail) must be managed directly with the provider.

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: What Hotel Accommodations am I Allowed to Book?
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
What Hotel Accommodations am I Allowed to Book?
What hotel accommodations am I allowed to book? Book the lowest refundable standard hotel room rate on a single-occupancy basis near your work destination through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) . All business travel must be booked within Amazon Travel & Events. Refer to section 5.0 Accomodation (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dec58a98-346b-920c-1662-8dbc2b8e05fb) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. Hotel location Book accommodations close to your work destination through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) or a Travel Counselor (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) . Hotel room type Amazon reimburses the lowest refundable standard hotel room rate on a single-occupancy basis. Why book Amazon preferred hotels Amazon negotiates with leading hotels conveniently located near Amazon offices. Preferred hotels offer: Competitive rates Flexible cancellation policies Loyalty program eligibility (not available in all countries or with all providers) Safety and security vetting While you may occasionally find a less expensive non-negotiated option, these rates may come with restrictions. Booking preferred rates contributes to overall cost savings across all travelers and provides greater flexibility if your plans change. Found a cheaper rate outside the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) ? Contact a Travel Counselor (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) to check if the same room/rate offer can be booked through Amazon Travel & Events. The offer must match the original room type and rate exactly. Long-term stay accommodation (7+ nights) When staying in the same location for 7 or more nights, Corporate Serviced Apartments can offer additional space, comfort, amenities, and savings. Unit types Select a studio apartment/flat or one-bedroom apartment/flat, whichever is less expensive. Approval requirements Long-term stay accommodation (Corporate Serviced Apartments only) requires L8+ approval. Shared economy properties The use of shared apartments or rental homes (including Airbnb) is strongly discouraged due to safety and security concerns. Use shared economy properties only when no suitable hotel or Corporate Serviced Apartment is available.

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: Can I earn personal miles or loyalty points when traveling for business?
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
Can I earn personal miles or loyalty points when traveling for business?
Can I earn personal miles or loyalty points when traveling for business? Yes. You can earn personal loyalty miles and points when traveling for business through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) . This applies to airlines, hotels, and rental cars. Refer to section 3.12 Loyalty Programs (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-12) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. Eligibility restrictions Employees based in Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Israel are not eligible to participate in supplier point programs due to payroll tax compliance requirements. How to earn miles Locate more information on the Amazon Travel & Events Preferred Travel Providers and Promotions Wiki (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/BusinessTravelPromotions/) . Sign up for loyalty programs. Add your loyalty program numbers to your Travel Profile. Miles and rewards are automatically credited to your personal account after each trip. Important requirements Ensure your loyalty numbers are accurate in your Travel Profile to avoid missing credits. Your Travel Profile name and your loyalty program name must match exactly. Contact the travel provider directly if miles are not credited after your trip.

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: Why am I receiving a new hotel confirmation I did not request?
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
Why am I receiving a new hotel confirmation I did not request?
Why am I receiving a new hotel confirmation I did not request? Amazon Travel & Events (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/) uses automated auditing technology that monitors your hotel reservations after booking to find lower rates. When a better rate becomes available, the system automatically rebooks your hotel to capture savings. What stays the same Your hotel property Your check-in and check-out dates Your room type and preferences All other reservation details What changes Your confirmation number (you receive a new one) Your rate (it is lower) Why this happens Hotel rates fluctuate due to demand, cancellations, and promotional pricing. The automated system monitors for price drops and secures savings automatically. What you need to do No action is needed from you. Rebooking happens automatically. Some exemptions may apply based on specific booking circumstances. Always use your new confirmation number when checking in or contacting the hotel.

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: Are Mobile Phone Expenses Reimbursable? - Global
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
Are Mobile Phone Expenses Reimbursable? - Global
Are mobile phone expenses reimbursable? For employees L4-L8, Amazon reimburses up to $50 USD per month of your personal mobile phone base plan. Exceptions apply for L1-3 and L10+. How to get reimbursed for your mobile phone bill: When submitting in the Expense Tool (https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) , select "Mobile Phone" as the Expense Type, enter your total base plan cost, and indicate your business use percentage (25% for infrequent use, 50% for mixed use, 75% for significant use, or 100% for primarily work use). The system calculates your reimbursement automatically, capped at $50. You'll need to attach your full itemized bill showing your name and/or phone number. If you use your phone for 100% business purposes, Amazon recommends getting an Amazon-provided mobile phone (https://my.it.a2z.com/articles/hardware/company-mobile-device/) if it is available in your country. Need to expense extra charges beyond your base plan? You can get reimbursed up to $150/month for incremental work-related costs like international roaming, travel daily fees, or mobile hotspots when corporate Wi-Fi isn't available. Submit these using the "Travel Related – Incremental Mobile Fees" Expense Type with detailed line-item documentation. Consult Section 12.6 of the Amazon Global Travel and Expense Policy (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) before submitting your mobile phone expenses for reimbursement to ensure compliance and eligibility.

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: I Accidentally Used My Corporate Card for a Personal Expense. What Should I Do? - Global
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
I Accidentally Used My Corporate Card for a Personal Expense. What Should I Do? - Global
Repaying Personal Expenses (Emburse) If you accidentally used your corporate card for a personal expense, need to split an expense as partially personal, or otherwise need to repay Amazon, you will need to take the following steps to submit an expense report to reconcile the expense. See the How to Submit Expenses user guide (https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses) for how to navigate the Expense Tool (https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) . If you used your corporate card for personal use. Common Scenario: Hey, it happens, you accidentally swiped your corporate card instead of your personal card. Find the corporate card charge in the Expense Tool (https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet If the entire amount is personal and non-reimbursable, select the Expense Type “Personal Expense”. Fill out the required fields. Continue building your Expense Report and submit. If you need to split an expense as partially personal. Common Scenario: You purchased multiple items in the same transaction but some of the expenses are ineligible for reimbursement per policy. Find the corporate card charge in the Expense Tool (https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet or create a new expense by uploading the receipt. Apply the appropriate expense type as per the nature of the expense and fill out the required fields. Under Allocation, click Add Allocation and select “PERSONAL Non-Reimbursable Personal Expense” Adjust the amounts to reflect what amount is personal. Continue building your Expense Report and submit. If you were reimbursed for an expense in error and now need to pay back Amazon. Common Scenario: You created a corporate card expense from scratch instead of waiting for it to appear in Concur. The system treats it as an out-of-pocket reimbursement and sends money to your personal account (as listed in Amazon Touchstone). Meanwhile, the actual corporate card charge remains "unsubmitted", which can lead to reminder emails - and even card suspension - if not corrected. Find the corporate card charge in the Expense Tool (https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet. If the entire amount is personal and non-reimbursable, select the Expense Type “Personal Expense” Fill out the required fields and add a comment explaining your previous incorrect submission, including the original expense report number. Continue building your Expense Report and submit. What Happens Next When you submit expenses as personal, you authorize Amazon to deduct the amount from future reimbursements or the account you have set up in Amazon Touchstone (https://touchstone.amazon.com/) . Repeated personal use on a corporate card may result in card suspension, so use your corporate card carefully. For additional guidance visit the Employee Expense & Corporate Card RIVER (https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER) .

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: Where Can I Locate My Previously Booked Trips? (Global Access)
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
Where Can I Locate My Previously Booked Trips? (Global Access)
Where can I locate my previously booked trips? Select Trips from the main menu in the Self-Service Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to access current and past trips, including invoices. Watch this quick video: Navigating My Trips (https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1804201) . Archived or missing trips If a trip is no longer visible or has been archived, open a request through Travel RIVER (https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to access your historical booking records. P rovider-managed bookings Bookings made directly through travel providers (including rail) must be managed directly with the provider.

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: Are Meals Covered During Business Travel? - Global
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
Are Meals Covered During Business Travel? - Global
Are meals covered during business travel? Amazon has you covered for meals and snacks while you're traveling away from home for business. Per diem and regional guidance Visit the Travel Reimbursement Guide (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Concur_Global_Expense/Travel_Reimbursement/) to learn more about per diems and region-specific guidance for expense limits what is eligible for meal reimbursement when you travel. Tips for meal expense compliance Retain the itemized receipt that includes a breakout of the individual meal items to later submit with your expense report. For team meals when traveling , the most senior person should pick up the tab and list everyone who attended when submitting the expense. Only split the bill if each person plans to expense their own meal separately. Additional rules may apply if your meal includes a government official. Review the Gift Reporting Guidelines (https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/legal-bce/GiftReportingGuidelines) and Section 12.12 of Amazon's Global Travel and Expense Policy (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#12.1) and consult with your manager before you entertain a government official.

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: How Do I Set Up My Travel Profile? (India)
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
How Do I Set Up My Travel Profile? (India)
How do I set up my Travel Profile? You must create a Travel Profile in the Self-Service Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) before booking business travel. Your Travel Profile securely stores your personal information, passport details, payment method, and travel preferences. Refer to section 3.4 Traveler Profiles (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#52c58a98-3509-0d43-4f83-20fcd24c2fec) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. What your Travel Profile stores Personal information: mobile phone number and date of birth (varies by country) Passport details (for international travel) Corporate or personal credit card (required to secure hotel reservations) Travel preferences and loyalty program numbers (optional) Important: Some information will be pre-populated for you—verify that your name exactly matches your government-issued ID. If your name exceeds 58 characters (the 58-character limit includes: salutation, space/dot , first name, middle name, and last name), you will need to book travel arrangements with a Travel Counselor. You will not be able to make travel arrangements online. Quick start guides Travel Profile Quick Start Guide for Travelers (India) (https://amazon.sharepoint.com/sites/AmazonTravelEventsPublicResources/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public%2FAmazon%5FTripsource%5FIndia%5FProfile%5FQSG%2D2025%2DFINAL%20%281%29%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public&p=true&ga=1) Travel Profile Quick Start Guide for Travel Arrangers (India) (https://amazon.sharepoint.com/sites/AmazonTravelEventsPublicResources/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public%2FAmazon%5FTripsource%5FIndia%5FTravel%20Arranger%5FQSG%2D2025%2DFINAL%20%281%29%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FAmazonTravelEventsPublicResources%2FShared%20Documents%2FTravel%20Public%2FIndia%20Public&p=true&ga=1) How to book after profile setup Standard bookings: Log in to the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and search for flights, hotels, car rentals, or rail options. Last-minute bookings (within 6 hours of departure): Contact a Travel Counselor (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) during business hours. For same-day travel outside the 6-hour window, book online through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) .

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: Why can I not see the Amazon form of payment when booking my flight?
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
Why can I not see the Amazon form of payment when booking my flight?
Why can I not see the Amazon form of payment when booking my flight? If you cannot select the Amazon form of payment (Amazon Central Bill Solution), your booking likely includes one or more personal or ancillary charges that are not eligible for central billing. Common reasons the Amazon form of payment is unavailable Personal upgrades (for example, premium cabin, extra legroom) Seat selection fees Baggage fees Other ancillary charges Personal travel segments Why this matters The Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) does not support the Amazon form of payment for personal add-ons or ancillary services. You must pay these using an alternate payment method. Additional consideration Some airlines require multi-factor authentication for payment processing. This also requires an alternate form of payment. Need further assistance If you believe you should have access to the Amazon form of payment and none of these conditions apply, contact Amazon Travel Support (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: How Do I Find the Right Expense Type? - Global
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
How Do I Find the Right Expense Type? - Global
How do I find the right expense type? The Expense Type Guide ( Concur (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Expense_Type/) , Emburse (https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/expense-type-guide) ) contains detailed descriptions of every expense type available for reimbursement in the Expense Tool ( Concur (https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) , Emburse (https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) ), including country-specific restrictions. If you don’t see the expense type you’re looking for in the Expense Tool ( Concur (https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) , Emburse (https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) ), then that expense isn’t available to you based on your location.

When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.title: How do I report a car accident while traveling for business?
When I'm traveling in EMEA or APAC and t.text:
How do I report a car accident while traveling for business?
How do I report a car accident while traveling for business? Contact emergency services first if there are injuries or immediate safety concerns. Then visit Amazon Travel Risk Management (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelRiskManagement/Home/) for the damage reporting process. Once you have completed these steps, inform your manager. Refer to section 6.8 Car Accidents (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#62c58a98-3514-2159-75a6-abd568753d90) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

report car accident rental business trav.title: How do I report a car accident while traveling for business?
report car accident rental business trav.text:
How do I report a car accident while traveling for business?
How do I report a car accident while traveling for business? Contact emergency services first if there are injuries or immediate safety concerns. Then visit Amazon Travel Risk Management (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelRiskManagement/Home/) for the damage reporting process. Once you have completed these steps, inform your manager. Refer to section 6.8 Car Accidents (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#62c58a98-3514-2159-75a6-abd568753d90) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

report car accident rental business trav.title: What size rental car am I allowed to book?
report car accident rental business trav.text:
What size rental car am I allowed to book?
What size rental car am I allowed to book? Book up to intermediate size vehicles (mini, economy, or compact) through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) . Refer to section 6.1 Rental Car Vehicle Size (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#6) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. Electric and hybrid vehicles Amazon encourages the use of electric or hybrid rental vehicles where available. If the only electric or hybrid vehicles available are standard or full-size, you may book these instead of an intermediate size vehicle. Exceptions for larger vehicles Standard, full-size cars, intermediate SUVs, or vans are permitted in the following situations: Three (3) or more travelers traveling together and sharing the rental vehicle Large work equipment requiring additional cargo space Pre-approved medical reasons Safety exceptions (for example, AWD/4WD in winter in locations prone to blizzards) Safe driving requirements Consider the risks associated with driving, especially outside your home country and after long flights. Driver requirements Hold a driving license suitable for the type of car rented and permitted by local law. Be familiar with local driving conditions before renting. Risk management Car rental is not advised unless you are familiar with local conditions. Follow Travel Risk Management (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelRiskManagement/Home/) (TRM) ground transportation recommendations for elevated risk destinations in TRM advisories.

report car accident rental business trav.title: Do I Need Rental Car Insurance for Work Travel?
report car accident rental business trav.text:
Do I Need Rental Car Insurance for Work Travel?
Do I need rental car insurance for work travel? Amazon provides Collision Damage Waiver (CDW) and Liability Damage Waiver (LDW) insurance for business rentals when you book through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and use preferred suppliers. Refer to section 6.2 Car Rental Insurance (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#88c58a98-3515-6808-3fb9-b6456aec9e1b) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. What is covered Collision Damage Waiver (CDW) Liability Damage Waiver (LDW) Deductibles may apply based on rental location What to do at the rental counter Decline additional insurance at the rental counter unless legally required and not already included in your rate.

report car accident rental business trav.title: My Expense Report Was Rejected. What Do I Do Now? - Global
report car accident rental business trav.text:
My Expense Report Was Rejected. What Do I Do Now? - Global
My expense report was rejected. What do I do now? Most expense reports are processed and paid within 4 business days. If your submitted expense report has been pending with no movement for more than 3 business days, it's likely been flagged for additional review. If your expense report is rejected: Rejected expense reports will be returned to you in the Expense Tool (https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) with guidance on how to fix and resubmit the expense report. You may be asked to submit legible receipts, provide additional justification for the expense, select a different expense type, or take other steps to fix the report. Review the Global Travel and Expense Policy (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) and if you believe your expense was rejected in error, share this feedback with a detailed justification in the comment field of the expense and resubmit the expense report. If your expense report requires additional approvals: Reviewers such as your manager or skip-level manager will be added to your expense report. No action is needed from you at this stage unless you choose to follow up with the reviewer to provide additional context. For assistance with resubmitting rejected expenses, contact the Amazon Global Expense Team through the Expense & Corporate Cards RIVER (https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER) .

report car accident rental business trav.title: What Should I Do if I Lost a Receipt? - Global
report car accident rental business trav.text:
What Should I Do if I Lost a Receipt? - Global
What do I do if I lost a receipt? If you are missing a required receipt for an expense report, you have three options: 1. Contact the merchant to request a new receipt. This option provides the best documentation, prevents reimbursement delays, and is the only acceptable solution for hotel stays, airfare, and car rentals. 2. Submit a bank/card statement if you can't get a merchant receip t. Include your name, transaction amount, and date (while redacting personal details and unrelated charges). This expense will require manager approval. 3. Use a Missing Receipt Declaration (MRD). This is a last resort for low-value expenses only and is not allowed for airfare, hotels, or car rentals. You must complete this yourself (delegates cannot) by selecting the checkbox "Missing Receipt" when you create the expense in the Expense Tool. Expenses with an MRD requires manager approval and repeated use may trigger an audit or restricted access. Learn more about receipt requirements found under Section 2.2 of the Global Travel and Expense Policy (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) . For further assistance, visit the Employee Expense & Corporate Card RIVER (https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER) .

report car accident rental business trav.title: What Is the Deadline for Submitting Expenses? - Global
report car accident rental business trav.text:
What Is the Deadline for Submitting Expenses? - Global
What is the deadline for submitting expenses? The Global Travel and Expense Policy (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) requires employees submit expense reports in the Expense Tool (https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) within 60 days of the transaction date. This applies to both out-of-pocket expenses (paid with cash or using your own credit card) and corporate card transactions (which will automatically populate in the Expense Tool (https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) within 5 business days). If you fail to submit corporate card transactions within 60 days, your corporate card may be suspended. Resubmitting if your expense report is rejected Most employee expense reports are processed and paid within 4 business days. If your expense report requires additional approvals or review, it could take longer than usual to be fully processed. Should you need to resubmit your expense report if it was initially rejected, the clock does not restart against the 60-day deadline. Need help? Review the How to Submit Expenses User Guide (https://w.amazon.com/bin/Concur_Global_Expense/User_Guide) before you submit an expense report to prevent errors that might lead to delays in reimbursement.

report car accident rental business trav.title: Are Meals Covered During Business Travel? - Global
report car accident rental business trav.text:
Are Meals Covered During Business Travel? - Global
Are meals covered during business travel? Amazon has you covered for meals and snacks while you're traveling away from home for business. Per diem and regional guidance Visit the Travel Reimbursement Guide (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Concur_Global_Expense/Travel_Reimbursement/) to learn more about per diems and region-specific guidance for expense limits what is eligible for meal reimbursement when you travel. Tips for meal expense compliance Retain the itemized receipt that includes a breakout of the individual meal items to later submit with your expense report. For team meals when traveling , the most senior person should pick up the tab and list everyone who attended when submitting the expense. Only split the bill if each person plans to expense their own meal separately. Additional rules may apply if your meal includes a government official. Review the Gift Reporting Guidelines (https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/legal-bce/GiftReportingGuidelines) and Section 12.12 of Amazon's Global Travel and Expense Policy (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#12.1) and consult with your manager before you entertain a government official.

report car accident rental business trav.title: How Long Does It Take to Get Reimbursed? - Global (Copy)
report car accident rental business trav.text:
How Long Does It Take to Get Reimbursed? - Global (Copy)
How long does it take to get reimbursed? Most expense reports are processed and paid within 4 business days. Why your expense report may take longer to process: If your submitted expense report has been pending with no movement for more than 3 business days, it's likely been flagged for additional review. If your expense report is rejected: Rejected expense reports will be returned to you in the Expense Tool (https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) with guidance on how to fix and resubmit the expense report. You may be asked to submit legible receipts, provide additional justification for the expense, select a different expense type, or take other steps to fix the report. Review the Global Travel and Expense Policy (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) and if you believe your expense was rejected in error, share this feedback with a detailed justification in the comment field of the expense and resubmit the expense report. If your expense report requires additional approvals: Reviewers such as your manager or skip-level manager will be added to your expense report. No action is needed from you at this stage unless you choose to follow up with the reviewer to provide additional context. Need Help? To avoid delays in reimbursement, review the Expense Report Checklist (https://amazon.awsapps.com/workdocs-amazon/index.html#/document/4e8b704d9b86af13c5587f92386f137d471dd3c267888f6cd7c0bf3070540cae) to ensure your report is complete, compliant, and accurate before you submit. For assistance with an expense report, contact the Amazon Global Expense Team through the Employee Expenses & Corporate Cards RIVER (https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER)

report car accident rental business trav.title: What Hotel Accommodations am I Allowed to Book?
report car accident rental business trav.text:
What Hotel Accommodations am I Allowed to Book?
What hotel accommodations am I allowed to book? Book the lowest refundable standard hotel room rate on a single-occupancy basis near your work destination through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) . All business travel must be booked within Amazon Travel & Events. Refer to section 5.0 Accomodation (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dec58a98-346b-920c-1662-8dbc2b8e05fb) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. Hotel location Book accommodations close to your work destination through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) or a Travel Counselor (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) . Hotel room type Amazon reimburses the lowest refundable standard hotel room rate on a single-occupancy basis. Why book Amazon preferred hotels Amazon negotiates with leading hotels conveniently located near Amazon offices. Preferred hotels offer: Competitive rates Flexible cancellation policies Loyalty program eligibility (not available in all countries or with all providers) Safety and security vetting While you may occasionally find a less expensive non-negotiated option, these rates may come with restrictions. Booking preferred rates contributes to overall cost savings across all travelers and provides greater flexibility if your plans change. Found a cheaper rate outside the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) ? Contact a Travel Counselor (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) to check if the same room/rate offer can be booked through Amazon Travel & Events. The offer must match the original room type and rate exactly. Long-term stay accommodation (7+ nights) When staying in the same location for 7 or more nights, Corporate Serviced Apartments can offer additional space, comfort, amenities, and savings. Unit types Select a studio apartment/flat or one-bedroom apartment/flat, whichever is less expensive. Approval requirements Long-term stay accommodation (Corporate Serviced Apartments only) requires L8+ approval. Shared economy properties The use of shared apartments or rental homes (including Airbnb) is strongly discouraged due to safety and security concerns. Use shared economy properties only when no suitable hotel or Corporate Serviced Apartment is available.

report car accident rental business trav.title: Why is my booking out of policy?
report car accident rental business trav.text:
Why is my booking out of policy?
Why is my booking out of policy? The "Out of Policy" flag in the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) helps you identify options that comply with the Global Travel and Expense Policy (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#1) . It does not prevent you from completing your booking. What the Global Travel and Expense Policy considers Lowest Logical Fare in economy with a seat assignment Lowest hotel rates Preferred car rates for the approved car type Out of Policy vs. Policy Violation Out of Policy: Your selection exceeds policy guidelines but may be justified for business reasons Yes — provide a reason for your selection. Policy Violation: Your selection violates a hard rule (for example, booking first class). No — the booking cannot be completed. When an out-of-policy booking is justified You may proceed with an out-of-policy booking when there is a valid business reason. Examples include: A hotel that is out of policy but closer to your business meetings An intermediate SUV during inclement weather for safety reasons Provide a brief reason when prompted. This serves as an audit tool to ensure flexibility while maintaining safety and frugality.

report car accident rental business trav.title: Why do I have to book travel in the program?
report car accident rental business trav.text:
Why do I have to book travel in the program?
Why do I have to book travel in the program? Booking within Amazon Travel & Events (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/) is required. The program provides safety, cost savings, booking simplicity, sustainability, and service quality benefits that are not available on consumer booking sites. Benefits of booking within Amazon Travel & Events Safety and security: Amazon Travel & Events provide 24/7 emergency support with instant access to your travel details for quick assistance. All bookings are automatically registered for security monitoring and medical assistance worldwide through International SOS. Cost savings: Amazon Travel & Events negotiates exclusive discounts with preferred suppliers. You access corporate rates on 400+ airlines, millions of hotel rooms, and car rental agencies that are not available on consumer booking sites. Booking simplicity: Access over 400 airlines and millions of hotel rooms through pre-vetted travel suppliers who meet rigorous safety and service standards. Sustainability: In line with Amazon's commitment to The Climate Pledge, purposeful travel options such as lower-emission rail and eco-conscious hotels are available. Select sustainable travel options when available. Service quality: Pre-screened travel providers meet high standards for reliability and customer care. Before you book Review section 3.0 General Booking Guidelines (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#4ec58a98-347a-e36a-54e0-278512f5781b) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy before booking.

report car accident rental business trav.title: I Accidentally Used My Corporate Card for a Personal Expense. What Should I Do? - Global
report car accident rental business trav.text:
I Accidentally Used My Corporate Card for a Personal Expense. What Should I Do? - Global
Repaying Personal Expenses (Emburse) If you accidentally used your corporate card for a personal expense, need to split an expense as partially personal, or otherwise need to repay Amazon, you will need to take the following steps to submit an expense report to reconcile the expense. See the How to Submit Expenses user guide (https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses) for how to navigate the Expense Tool (https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) . If you used your corporate card for personal use. Common Scenario: Hey, it happens, you accidentally swiped your corporate card instead of your personal card. Find the corporate card charge in the Expense Tool (https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet If the entire amount is personal and non-reimbursable, select the Expense Type “Personal Expense”. Fill out the required fields. Continue building your Expense Report and submit. If you need to split an expense as partially personal. Common Scenario: You purchased multiple items in the same transaction but some of the expenses are ineligible for reimbursement per policy. Find the corporate card charge in the Expense Tool (https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet or create a new expense by uploading the receipt. Apply the appropriate expense type as per the nature of the expense and fill out the required fields. Under Allocation, click Add Allocation and select “PERSONAL Non-Reimbursable Personal Expense” Adjust the amounts to reflect what amount is personal. Continue building your Expense Report and submit. If you were reimbursed for an expense in error and now need to pay back Amazon. Common Scenario: You created a corporate card expense from scratch instead of waiting for it to appear in Concur. The system treats it as an out-of-pocket reimbursement and sends money to your personal account (as listed in Amazon Touchstone). Meanwhile, the actual corporate card charge remains "unsubmitted", which can lead to reminder emails - and even card suspension - if not corrected. Find the corporate card charge in the Expense Tool (https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet. If the entire amount is personal and non-reimbursable, select the Expense Type “Personal Expense” Fill out the required fields and add a comment explaining your previous incorrect submission, including the original expense report number. Continue building your Expense Report and submit. What Happens Next When you submit expenses as personal, you authorize Amazon to deduct the amount from future reimbursements or the account you have set up in Amazon Touchstone (https://touchstone.amazon.com/) . Repeated personal use on a corporate card may result in card suspension, so use your corporate card carefully. For additional guidance visit the Employee Expense & Corporate Card RIVER (https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER) .

report car accident rental business trav.title: How Do I Set Up My Travel Profile? (Japan)
report car accident rental business trav.text:
How Do I Set Up My Travel Profile? (Japan)
How do I set up my Travel Profile? You must create a Travel Profile in the Self-Service Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) before booking business travel. Your Travel Profile securely stores your personal information, passport details, payment method, and travel preferences. Refer to section 3.4 Traveler Profiles (https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#52c58a98-3509-0d43-4f83-20fcd24c2fec) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy. What your Travel Profile stores Personal information: mobile phone number and date of birth (varies by country) Passport details (for international travel) Corporate or personal credit card (required to secure hotel reservations) Travel preferences and loyalty program numbers (optional) Quick start videos Travel Quick Start Videos (Japan) (https://broadcast.amazon.com/channels/1303/playlists/18326) How to book after profile setup Standard bookings: Log in to the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and search for flights, hotels, car rentals, or rail options. Last-minute bookings (within 6 hours of departure): Contact a Travel Counselor (https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) during business hours. For same-day travel outside the 6-hour window, book online through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) .
